task_completion.description: Open and review the maintenance specification document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or document viewer open on the screen displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/fm_maintenance_spec.txt'.

task_completion.description: Model a boiler (IfcBoiler) and a pump (IfcPump) on the ground floor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's 3D viewport or outliner showing newly added objects representing a boiler and a pump on the ground floor, with BlenderBIM object properties indicating 'IfcBoiler' and 'IfcPump' IFC classes.
BlenderBIM's sequencing/scheduling panel showing a new work schedule named 'Annual PPM Schedule'.

task_completion.description: Create the specified maintenance tasks under the schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The schedule tree in BlenderBIM showing exactly 2 tasks created (e.g., 'Boiler Annual Service' and 'Pump Inspection').

task_completion.description: Assign each piece of equipment to its respective task.
task_completion.visual_evidence: BlenderBIM task assignment panels or properties showing the 'IfcBoiler' linked to the boiler task and the 'IfcPump' linked to the pump task (establishing the process-to-product relationship).

task_completion.description: Save the FM-ready model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's save/export dialog or a file manager showing the file being saved as 'fzk_fm_maintenance.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/BlenderBIM GUI to model equipment and create schedules.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with Blender's 3D viewport, outliner, and BlenderBIM add-on panels, rather than executing a Python script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using Blender's export/save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender file browser is visible when saving the '.ifc' file, rather than a terminal 'cp' or 'mv' command creating a dummy file.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the schedule creation in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The schedule and tasks are clearly visible within the BlenderBIM interface panels, not just typed out in a text editor or terminal window.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The maintenance specification document is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/fm_maintenance_spec.txt'. The expected schedule name is 'Annual PPM Schedule'. The agent must create exactly 2 tasks (Boiler Annual Service, Pump Inspection) and model two equipment types: 'IfcBoiler' and 'IfcPump'. The final model must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_fm_maintenance.ifc'.
